# gmaps-reviews-short-video

> Generate a 7-second 1080×1920 short-form video from a Google Maps business URL,
> combining a Ken Burns photo slideshow, star-rating overlay, top customer reviews,
> and optional background music — ready for Instagram Reels, TikTok, or YouTube Shorts.

Source: https://github.com/alx/gmaps-reviews-short-video

## What this tool does

Given a Google Maps business URL, the tool fetches the place's name, rating, review
count, photos, and top reviews via the Google Maps Places API (New). It then composes a
7-second portrait video (H.264, 1080×1920) with a Ken Burns zoom slideshow of up to 5
photos, a semi-transparent header showing the star rating and business name, up to 3
customer review cards timed across the video, and a 2-second outro card with the
business name and website. Optionally adds background music with fade-in/out, and can
upload the finished video directly to YouTube.

## Setup

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package manager
- `ffmpeg` installed and available on `PATH`
- A Google Maps Platform API key with **Places API (New)** enabled

### One-time setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/alx/gmaps-reviews-short-video.git
cd gmaps-reviews-short-video
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and set GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
uv sync
```

### .env file

```
GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=<your_google_maps_api_key>
YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRETS=client_secrets.json   # only needed for --publish
```

## CLI usage

```bash
uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "<google_maps_url>"
```

### Arguments

| Argument | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | YES | — | Google Maps business URL (full or shortened) |
| `--output FILE` | no | `output/output.mp4` | Output MP4 path |
| `--music FILE` | no | _(none)_ | Path to MP3/WAV for background music |
| `--publish` | no | off | Upload to YouTube after generation |

### Examples

```bash
# Minimal — just the URL
uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..."

# Custom output path with background music
uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..." \
  --output output/my_business.mp4 \
  --music mp3/alec_koff-carnaval-484622.mp3

# Generate and publish to YouTube
uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..." \
  --music mp3/alec_koff-carnaval-484622.mp3 \
  --publish
```

A bundled royalty-free music track is at `mp3/alec_koff-carnaval-484622.mp3`.

## Output

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Format | MP4 (H.264 video, AAC audio when music provided) |
| Resolution | 1080×1920 (portrait / 9:16) |
| Duration | 7 seconds |
| Frame rate | 30 fps |
| Default path | `output/output.mp4` |

The output directory (`output/`) is included in the repo with a `.gitkeep`.

## Key source files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/main.py` | CLI entry point — argparse, env loading, orchestration |
| `src/gmaps.py` | Google Maps Places API v1 client — place ID extraction, photo download, review filtering |
| `src/video.py` | MoviePy video composition engine — Ken Burns, review cards, outro, header |
| `src/youtube.py` | YouTube Data API v3 upload with OAuth 2.0 and token caching |
| `.env.example` | Environment variable template |
| `pyproject.toml` | Project metadata, dependencies, entry-point declaration |

## Common tasks for AI agents

1. **Generate a video (no music)**
   ```bash
   uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..."
   ```

2. **Generate a video with background music**
   ```bash
   uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..." \
     --music mp3/alec_koff-carnaval-484622.mp3
   ```

3. **Generate and immediately publish to YouTube**
   ```bash
   uv run gmaps-reviews-short-video "https://www.google.com/maps/place/..." \
     --music mp3/alec_koff-carnaval-484622.mp3 \
     --publish
   ```

4. **Run tests**
   ```bash
   uv sync --group dev
   uv run pytest
   ```

5. **Install dependencies after cloning**
   ```bash
   uv sync
   ```

## Video composition details

- **Background:** up to 5 business photos, each with 1.08× Ken Burns zoom, 0.5 s crossfades
- **Header (top 150 px):** semi-transparent dark bar — star rating + business name (truncated to 32 chars)
- **Review cards:** up to 3 cards, time-distributed — author, star rating, review text (truncated to 240 chars); only reviews with ≥ 4 stars and ≥ 60-character text are included
- **Outro card (last 2 s):** business name + website URL, centered with fade-in
- **Global fade:** 0.5 s fade-in from black and fade-out to black

## YouTube publishing (optional)

### Setup

1. Enable **YouTube Data API v3** in Google Cloud Console
2. Create **OAuth 2.0 credentials** (Desktop app type) → download `client_secrets.json`
3. Add to `.env`:
   ```
   YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRETS=client_secrets.json
   ```
4. First run opens a browser for OAuth consent; token is cached at
   `~/.config/reviewreel/token.json` for all subsequent runs

### Published video properties

- **Title:** `{business_name} — Google Maps Reviews`
- **Category:** People & Blogs (22)
- **Tags:** `google maps`, `reviews`, `local business`
- **Privacy:** Public
- **Description:** includes rating, review count, Google Maps URL, website URL, and a
  link back to this open-source project

## URL handling

- Full Google Maps URLs with place IDs (`ChIJ…` format): extracted directly
- Shortened URLs (`goo.gl`, `maps.app`): resolved via HTTP redirect
- URLs without a place ID: falls back to text search by business name

## Environment variables reference

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` | YES | Google Maps Platform API key (Places API New) |
| `YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRETS` | only for `--publish` | Path to OAuth 2.0 Desktop credentials JSON |
